summary: chai-chain-dom fetches and executes remote JavaScript from api.npoint.io on require()
details: On require() of chai-chain-dom, index.js spawns a detached child process running lib/caller.js. caller.js fetches JavaScript from https://api.npoint.io/b9622a4b0e40dc0ae722 and evaluates the returned `data.model` string via `new Function.constructor('require', src)(require)`, retrying up to 5 times. The remote endpoint is a third-party paste service whose contents are mutable and controlled by whoever published the package, giving that operator arbitrary code execution in the installer's Node process. lib/const.js additionally stores base64-encoded strings that decode to a fallback loader URL (https://jsonkeeper.com/b/ZK45J) plus an `x-secret-key` header, matching the same remote-fetch-and-execute shape. The package presents itself as a pino-compatible logger (README reproduces pino's badges and prose, `module.exports.pino = middleware`, internal `require('./pino')`) to increase the odds of accidental installation, but ships no logging functionality.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
